problem:
The scores on a $110$-point test were organized in the stem-and-leaf plot shown. $9 | 6$ represents $96$ points. What is the mode of the scores? \begin{tabular}{c|lllllll}
\multicolumn{8}{c}{\underline{Points on the Test}}\\
5 &0 & 0 & & & & &\\
6 &3 & & & & & &\\
7 &7 & 8 & & & & &\\
8 &2 & 6 & 7 & 9 & 9 & 9 & 9\\
9 &1 & 4 & 4 & 4 & 6 & &\\
10 &0 & 0 & 0 & & & &\\
\end{tabular}

classified_questions.question: Can you explain what the stem-and-leaf plot is used for in organizing test scores?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: What do the numbers in each row of the stem-and-leaf plot represent?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How can you determine the frequency of a particular score from the stem-and-leaf plot?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Which row in the stem-and-leaf plot has the highest frequency for a particular digit, and what does that tell us about the mode?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why the mode is determined by the row with the highest frequency of a particular digit rather than just the digit itself?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: If there were multiple rows with the same highest frequency, how would you determine the mode in that case?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How would you use the stem-and-leaf plot to find the median or mean of the scores?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Are there any limitations or drawbacks to using a stem-and-leaf plot to organize test scores?
classified_questions.label: b
